statement.en: Novel roles of SINHCAF in hypoxia
statement.summary: Cells constantly adapt to changes in their environment, especially during stress conditions like low oxygen (hypoxia). This process requires major changes in gene activity and DNA packaging, a system largely controlled by chromatin regulatory proteins. One such protein, SINHCAF (SIN3A and HDAC-associated factor), has long been thought to act only as a gene repressor, helping turn genes off by tightening the structure of chromatin. SINHCAF has traditionally been recognized as a component of the Sin3A-HDAC complex and predominantly associated with transcriptional repression through chromatin compaction via histone deacetylation. In this study, SINHCAF was hypothesised to play a key role in gene silencing and maintaining chromatin compaction, especially in response to hypoxia. Therefore, this

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Preprint dated 2026-04-25 with incomplete abstract (truncated mid-sentence). Hypothesis-stage work on SINHCAF hypoxia response. Not yet peer-reviewed or validated. Provisional claim that SINHCAF has 'novel roles' requires experimental validation.
